mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 4.33% suggests room for easing if inflation moderates
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration that increases recession risk, triggering equity risk premium expansion and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 4.2% is near trigger threshold
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market softening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 1324K and RSAFS at 724131M show recent momentum
failure_condition: Interaction term (RSAFS × HOUST) produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below its 3-month moving average signals supply-side weakness and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 103.88 reflects recent manufacturing activity
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 65% of instances where INDPRO is below its 3-month MA, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint expansion in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and a declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals strong labor market momentum that supports consumer spending and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS grows > 150K month-over-month AND UNRATE declines by > 0.1pp
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 159517K and UNRATE at 4.2% show labor market resilience
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with strong labor demand
failure_condition: Interaction term (PAYEMS growth × UNRATE decline) produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day returns are negative in > 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated, monetary policy is restrictive and equity valuations compress, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 4.33% and CPIAUCSL at 320.321 indicate tight monetary conditions
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with sticky inflation
failure_condition: Joint restrictive signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerating above 2.5% annualized rate signals robust economic expansion that supports corporate earnings and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 23526.085B reflects recent economic activity
regime_hint: mid-to-late cycle expansion with above-trend growth
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of instances where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) and rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals stagflationary pressure that compresses equity risk appetite and valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month-over-month AND CPIAUCSL month-over-month change > 0.3%
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 103.88 and CPIAUCSL at 320.321 reflect supply-demand imbalances
failure_condition: Stagflation signal produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances.
